symbols: soldiers with weapons — force against the Prince of Peace
bound Christ — voluntary submission to suffering
crowd — humanity's complicity in Christ's suffering
description: A wide, frieze-like Renaissance painting showing the arrest of Christ in Gethsemane. A crowd of armed soldiers and figures seizes Christ, who is bound and being led away. The composition shows intense physical interaction — pushing, grabbing, and restraining. Soldiers wear ancient armor and carry weapons. The scene is crowded and violent, painted in Signorelli's muscular, dynamic style with a landscape background.
theological_description: The arrest of Christ in the Garden of Gethsemane marks the beginning of the Passion proper. After Judas's betrayal with a kiss, a crowd armed with swords and clubs seizes Jesus. Despite his ability to call upon legions of angels, Christ submits willingly to arrest, declaring, 'This has all taken place that the writings of the prophets might be fulfilled.' The violence of the arrest contrasts with Christ's peaceful surrender, embodying the paradox of the Passion — divine power expressed through voluntary weakness. The soldiers' aggression represents the blindness of worldly power confronting divine love.
